data.description:
## Context
Both the plan-review gate (`5fd2`) Layer-1 DET floor and the three-pass code reviewers (`9da1`) need to ground
findings in the actual code: resolve files/symbols/imports, confirm a dependency exists, and measure size —
deterministically, polyglot, and fail-open. NONE of this tooling is wired into rebar today. Building it once,
as a shared library capability, avoids two divergent implementations and keeps the reviewers consistent.

## Success Criteria
- A reusable internal API exposes: file/symbol/import resolution (LSP via solidlsp / ast-grep), dependency
  existence (syft + per-ecosystem registry + LLM-fallback), and size/complexity metrics (scc / lizard).
- Every wrapper FAILS OPEN: unsupported language / missing server / crash / timeout -> "skipped" (never a
  false negative), with the skip recorded so callers can note coverage.
- Consumed by `5fd2`'s DET floor (P2/P3/P4) and the `9da1` code reviewers; one implementation, not two.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File/symbol/import resolution wrapper (LSP/ast-grep), polyglot, fail-open, tested (unsupported-lang / no-server / crash / timeout -> skipped).
- [ ] Dependency-existence wrapper (syft + registry + fallback) and size/complexity wrapper (scc/lizard), fail-open, tested.
- [ ] A stable internal API both review gates consume; `5fd2` and `9da1` depend on this epic.
data.title: Code-grounding tooling (prerequisite): polyglot deterministic file/symbol/dependency/size resolution, fail-open
